nameMeanings.name: Fortescue
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French fort ‘strong’ + escu ‘shield’.
History: A family of this name claim descent from Radulfus Fortescu who is recorded in Domesday Book (1086) as holding lands at Modbury (Devon).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Fortescue was born on 1 January 1502, in England, her father, Adrian Fortescue, was 26 and her mother, Anne Stonor, was 18. She married Thomas Wentworth in 1520, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 6 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Fortescue est née le 1 janvier 1502, à Angleterre, son père, Adrian Fortescue, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Anne Stonor, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Wentworth en 1520, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 6 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Fortescue am 1. Januar 1502 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Adrian Fortescue 26 und ihre Mutter Anne Stonor 18. Sie hat 1520 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Thomas Wentworth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 6 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Fortescue è nata il il 1 gennaio 1502 nel England, sua padre, Adrian Fortescue, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Anne Stonor, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Wentworth nel 1520, nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 6 figlie.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Fortescue nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1502, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Adrian Fortescue, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Anne Stonor, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Wentworth em 1520, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Fortescue родилась 1 января 1502 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Adrian Fortescue было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Stonor, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Wentworth в 1520 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Fortescue nació el 1 de enero de 1502, en Inglaterra, su papá, Adrian Fortescue, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Anne Stonor, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Thomas Wentworth en 1520, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 6 hijas.
